title: feat: add community model activation toggle
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds Deactivate and Reactivate actions to My Models.
- Reuses the existing model update API and disabled fields; no migration or new endpoint.
- Lets model owners pause and resume listings without editing configuration.
- Validated with Biome, Enter and Gen typechecks, and the focused community-endpoint integration test.

gist.headline: Models can take a nap
gist.blurb: Community model owners can now tuck a listing away and bring it back when the timing is right. No configuration surgery required.
gist.summary: Added Deactivate and Reactivate actions to My Models, letting community model owners toggle an existing listing's disabled state through the current model update API.
gist.impact: Model owners can now pause a community listing and resume it later without editing its configuration or creating a new model entry.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polli as a gentle garden keeper using a small toggle switch to let a code-flower model rest beneath a cozy shade cover, while a second blooming code-flower is reactivated with warm light. Include a retro My Models terminal, a clear ACTIVE/RESTING status motif, and small vines and flowers to represent listings remaining safely intact.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny gardener hat, gently flipping a chunky toggle on a retro "MY MODELS" terminal: one code-flower listing rests under a little shade canopy while another blooms back to life. Prominent soft lime green (#ecf874), warm cream, mint and lavender pastel gradients, vines and pixel flowers, cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes; pixel-font text: "ACTIVE" and "RESTING".
